title: AI Data Centers in Southeast Asia
subtitle: Illustrative market scan generated with BankerDeck

slides.title: AI Data Centers in Southeast Asia
slides.subtitle: Market scan and investment implications

slides.title: SEA AI data center demand is attractive, but near-term power access will separate winners from followers
bullets: Hyperscale and AI workload growth are increasing demand for high-density, power-secure capacity.
Singapore constraints are pushing incremental demand toward Johor, Batam, Bangkok, Manila, and Ho Chi Minh City.
Investors should prioritize sites with contracted power, credible cooling design, and cloud/customer pre-commitments.
Key diligence gaps include grid queue position, land title, water access, and renewable procurement economics.
slides.source: Source: Public industry sources; company disclosures; BankerDeck analysis.

slides.title: Illustrative demand scenario shows accelerated capacity absorption under AI workload growth
slides.source: Note: Illustrative GW demand scenario. Source: BankerDeck analysis.

slides.title: Power, connectivity, and policy maturity are the primary market-selection filters
rows: Grid capacity, queue position, PPA path

slides.title: Recommended next step is a focused 6-week site and partner diligence sprint
